title: Supreme Court Recognises Right to Walk on Footpaths as Fundamental Right
summary: The Supreme Court of India has issued an order recognising the right of pedestrians to walk safely on footpaths as a fundamental right, amid a surge in pedestrian deaths across Indian cities. The ruling connects pedestrian safety to the right to life under Article 21 of the Constitution. The order comes in the backdrop of data showing India accounts for a disproportionately high share of global pedestrian fatalities. The judgment has prompted political parties, including the Congress, to seek a similar fundamental right status for the right to vote.
exam_angle: Tests knowledge of Article 21 (Right to Life) and its expansive judicial interpretation by the Supreme Court — a recurring UPSC theme. Also relevant for Urban Governance and Smart Cities topics in GS Paper 2.
key_facts: Right to walk on footpaths linked to Article 21 (Right to Life)
Supreme Court expanded the scope of fundamental rights via judicial interpretation
India has one of the highest pedestrian death rates globally
Congress subsequently demanded fundamental right status for the right to vote

title: India-EU Free Trade Agreement to be Signed by December 2025, Implemented by February-March 2026
summary: Union Minister Piyush Goyal announced that the India-EU Free Trade Agreement (FTA) is on track to be signed by December 2025, with implementation expected from February-March 2026. Negotiations between India and the European Union for a Bilateral Trade and Investment Agreement (BTIA) have been ongoing for nearly two decades. The agreement, if finalised, would be one of India's most significant trade pacts with a major economic bloc. Goyal also noted India is seeking tariff advantages over rival nations before committing to any trade deal with the US.
exam_angle: India-EU trade relations and FTA negotiations are frequently asked in GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Economy). The BTIA has been a longstanding negotiation since 2007 — aspirants should know its history and sticking points.
key_facts: India-EU FTA to be signed by December 2025
Implementation targeted for February-March 2026
Negotiations under the Bilateral Trade and Investment Agreement (BTIA) began in 2007
Minister Piyush Goyal made the announcement

title: US-Iran Nuclear Talks Begin in Switzerland with JD Vance and Iranian Officials
summary: US Vice President JD Vance and Iranian officials arrived in Switzerland to begin a new round of nuclear talks, signalling a renewed diplomatic effort to address Iran's nuclear programme. The talks are aimed at reaching a framework to prevent Iran from developing nuclear weapons in exchange for sanctions relief. Switzerland is acting as a neutral venue for the high-stakes diplomacy. These negotiations are significant amid ongoing tensions in the Middle East and concerns about nuclear proliferation.
exam_angle: Iran's nuclear programme, the JCPOA framework, and US-Iran relations are important for GS Paper 2 (IR) — especially in the context of India's energy security given India's historic oil imports from Iran.
key_facts: US-Iran nuclear talks held in Switzerland
US represented by Vice President JD Vance
Talks aimed at limiting Iran's nuclear programme in exchange for sanctions relief
Switzerland serving as neutral host venue

title: International Day of Yoga 2025: PM Modi Leads Celebrations from Kolkata; Theme is 'Yoga for One Earth, One Health'
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi led the nationwide International Day of Yoga 2025 celebrations from Kolkata, marking the 11th edition of the event since the UN designated June 21 as International Day of Yoga in 2015. President Droupadi Murmu described Yoga as 'India's gift to the world, guiding humanity amid challenges.' The Shanghai Bund event marked celebrations with a focus on the theme of healthy ageing. India proposed the International Day of Yoga at the UN General Assembly in 2014, which was adopted unanimously.
exam_angle: International Day of Yoga is relevant for GS Paper 1 (Culture/Heritage) and also for India's soft power diplomacy. The UN resolution, year of adoption, and India's role are important facts for the exam.
key_facts: June 21 is International Day of Yoga (IDY), designated by the UN in 2015
2025 marks the 11th International Day of Yoga
PM Modi led celebrations from Kolkata
India proposed IDY at UNGA in 2014; resolution adopted unanimously with record co-sponsors

title: A New Chapter in India's Nuclear Journey: PIB Highlights Nuclear Energy Expansion
summary: The Press Information Bureau (PIB) published a feature titled 'A New Chapter in India's Nuclear Journey,' highlighting the country's expanding nuclear energy programme. India is pushing to increase its nuclear power capacity as part of its clean energy and energy security goals. The government has been pursuing amendments to nuclear liability laws to attract private and foreign investment in the sector. India's civil nuclear programme operates under the Department of Atomic Energy.
exam_angle: India's nuclear energy programme, the Civil Liability for Nuclear Damage Act 2010, and the three-stage nuclear programme (Bhabha's plan) are key topics for GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech and Economy).
key_facts: PIB highlights India's expanding nuclear energy programme
Nuclear power governed by Department of Atomic Energy
India follows a three-stage nuclear programme (Pressurised Heavy Water → Fast Breeder → Thorium)
Civil Liability for Nuclear Damage Act 2010 governs liability framework

title: Centre Prioritising Energy Security Through Shift Towards Coal Gasification
summary: The Central Government has stated it is prioritising energy security by accelerating the shift towards coal gasification as an alternative to direct coal combustion. Coal gasification converts coal into syngas (synthesis gas), which can be used for power generation, fertiliser production, and as a substitute for imported natural gas. India holds the world's fourth-largest coal reserves, making coal gasification a strategic option for reducing import dependence. The government has set a target to gasify 100 million tonnes of coal by 2030.
exam_angle: Coal gasification is important for GS Paper 3 (Energy Security, Economy). India's coal reserves, import dependence for natural gas, and alternative energy technologies are recurring themes in UPSC mains.
key_facts: Government is shifting focus to coal gasification for energy security
Coal gasification produces syngas usable for power and fertilisers
India targets gasification of 100 million tonnes of coal by 2030
India has world's 4th largest coal reserves

title: Bangladesh's Tarique Rahman Embarks on First Foreign Tour: Malaysia First, Then China
summary: Tarique Rahman, Acting Chairman of Bangladesh Nationalist Party (BNP) and de facto leader of Bangladesh's interim political landscape, has embarked on his debut foreign tour, visiting Malaysia first and then China. This tour signals Bangladesh's diplomatic outreach under the post-Sheikh Hasina political arrangement. China has been a major infrastructure and economic partner for Bangladesh, and Tarique's visit to Beijing may reframe Bangladesh-China relations. India watches Bangladesh's foreign policy closely given shared borders and strategic interests.
exam_angle: Bangladesh's political developments and foreign policy realignments are important for GS Paper 2 (IR) — especially India's neighbourhood-first policy and China's growing influence in South Asia.
key_facts: Tarique Rahman is Acting Chairman of Bangladesh Nationalist Party (BNP)
First foreign tour: Malaysia followed by China
Tour signals Bangladesh's diplomatic reorientation post-Sheikh Hasina era
India-Bangladesh-China triangular dynamics are strategically significant

title: Delhi High Court Orders Centre to Remove 'False' Social Media Posts About CJI and Ministers
summary: The Delhi High Court directed the Central Government to take down social media posts falsely claiming that Chief Justice of India (CJI) and Union Ministers were playing badminton at an event in the United Kingdom. The court invoked concerns about misinformation targeting constitutional authorities. The order is significant as it touches on the intersection of free speech (Article 19), the right to reputation, and the state's responsibility to counter targeted disinformation. The government was directed to act through appropriate IT Act mechanisms.
exam_angle: Relevant for GS Paper 2 (Polity) on judicial oversight, IT Act/IT Rules 2021, freedom of speech vs. misinformation, and separation of powers. Also relevant for the debate on fake news regulation.
key_facts: Delhi High Court ordered removal of false posts about the CJI and Union Ministers
Posts falsely showed officials playing badminton at a UK event
Order invokes IT Act provisions for content takedown
Touches on Article 19 (free speech) vs. combating disinformation

title: Further Investigation by Police After Final Report: Supreme Court Clarifies Law
summary: The Supreme Court of India clarified the legal position on whether police require court permission to conduct further investigation after filing a final report (chargesheet) under the Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC). The Court addressed the interplay between Section 173(8) CrPC (further investigation) and the Magistrate's cognizance powers. The ruling has significant implications for criminal procedure and the rights of accused persons. With the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS) 2023 replacing CrPC, the corresponding provision is Section 193.
exam_angle: Criminal procedure law, specifically CrPC Section 173(8) / BNSS Section 193, and the Supreme Court's role in interpreting procedural law are relevant for GS Paper 2 (Polity/Governance) and also Law optional.
key_facts: Section 173(8) CrPC (now Section 193 BNSS) governs further investigation after final report
Supreme Court clarified police powers post-chargesheet
Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS) 2023 replaced CrPC
Ruling impacts rights of accused and magisterial oversight

title: Parliament Standing Committee to Review India's Economic Performance Amid Global Turmoil
summary: A Parliament Standing Committee is set to review India's macroeconomic performance in the context of global headwinds, including trade tensions, geopolitical conflicts, and growth concerns. The review will assess India's GDP trajectory, fiscal deficit, inflation, and export performance. Parliamentary standing committees play a critical oversight role in scrutinising government policies and economic management. The review comes at a time when global tariff wars and supply chain disruptions pose risks to India's growth targets.
exam_angle: Parliamentary Standing Committees are important for GS Paper 2 (Polity — Parliament) and GS Paper 3 (Economy). Their composition, powers, and role in financial oversight are frequently tested.
key_facts: Parliamentary Standing Committee to review India's economic performance
Focus areas: GDP growth, fiscal deficit, inflation, export performance
Standing Committees have oversight role over government ministries
Review context: global trade tensions and geopolitical disruptions

title: India Seeks Tariff Advantage Over Rivals Before Finalising US Trade Deal
summary: India is seeking assurances of preferential tariff treatment compared to rival nations — particularly China, Vietnam, and Bangladesh — before formalising a trade deal with the United States. The negotiations come amid US President Trump's tariff regime and India's interest in positioning itself as an alternative manufacturing hub. India faces a reciprocal tariff of 26% from the US, currently paused during a 90-day negotiation window. The outcome of these talks will have significant implications for India's export competitiveness, especially in textiles, electronics, and pharmaceuticals.
exam_angle: India-US trade negotiations, WTO-compatible trade agreements, and India's export competitiveness are key themes for GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Economy — Trade Policy).
key_facts: India seeking tariff edge over China, Vietnam, Bangladesh in US trade deal
US imposed 26% reciprocal tariff on India (currently paused for 90 days)
Talks ongoing during Trump administration's tariff negotiation window
Key sectors: textiles, electronics, pharmaceuticals
